version: v2 — real Bend Council meeting footage (Granicus archive)
topic: Bend climate pollution fee on natural gas in new residential construction

primary_sources.title: Bend City Council Work Session — Feb 11 2026 — Electrification Policy Process Fee Options (75-min agenda item)
primary_sources.license: Public record (Oregon Public Records Law). Government meeting recording produced by City of Bend. Used for news commentary.

clips_used.content: Cassie Lacy 'Proposed Fee Design' slide — SCC × Net Lifetime Carbon Produced × Tier Factor formula
'Impacts of Fee Levels' slide — $1,954 / $4,885 / $9,771 max per home; 0.23% / 0.58% / 1.15% housing cost impact
'Grid Impact Numbers' slide — 6.73 to 14.99 MW annual additional demand
'Recommended Approach to Policy Development' slide — Temporary Committee on Electrification Policy (TCEP) structure
Pacific Power 'Blueprint South 500 kV Transmission Project' slide

primary_sources.title: Bend City Council Business Meeting — Jan 7 2026 (joint committee on electrification appointment discussion)
primary_sources.license: Public record. Used for establishing Council Chambers footage.

clips_used.content: Public comment table + Council Chamber wide shot

primary_sources.title: Bend City Council Business Meeting — Feb 4 2026 (Economic Development Strategy adoption)
primary_sources.license: Public record. Used for Council member close-up.

clips_used.content: Councilor Franzosa close-up + Council Chamber inset

primary_sources.creator: Mick Haupt
primary_sources.license: Unsplash License (free use; on-screen credit in video)
primary_sources.geo_note: Central Oregon landscape road (asset library tagged bend, central-oregon)
used_in_compositions: BendPulsePart1 hook bridge under Victoria VO

verification_trace_per_figure.claim: Three fee levels: $1,954 / $4,885 / $9,771 max per home
verification_trace_per_figure.evidence: feb11_fee_levels.mp4 visually shows the slide with these exact numbers in the 'Impacts of Fee Levels' table
feb11_fee_levels.mp4 — same slide, 'Housing Cost Impact (%)' row

verification_trace_per_figure.claim: Fee applies only to new SFR / duplex / townhouse / ADU; existing homes off the table
verification_trace_per_figure.source_text: This policy would not apply to existing homes or buildings. ... The climate pollution fee under consideration would apply only to new residential construction.

verification_trace_per_figure.claim: Three industries showed up at BEDAB Jan 5 (COBA, Bend Chamber, UA Local 290)
verification_trace_per_figure.source_text: Garrett Mosher, on behalf of the Central Oregon Builders Association ... Dave DeBerger, on behalf of UA local 290 Plumbers and Steamfitters ... Sara Odendahl, on behalf of the Bend Chamber of Commerce

verification_trace_per_figure.claim: BEDAB voted 8-0 asking Council for a pilot
verification_trace_per_figure.source_text: Member Evons moved for BEDAB to draft a letter specifically focused on a pilot period with a data driven approach to validate a fee. Member Taylor seconded the motion. The voice vote passed 8-0. Member Kroncke did not vote.

verification_trace_per_figure.claim: Chair Gary North quote: social cost of unaffordable housing > social cost of carbon
verification_trace_per_figure.source_text: Chair North stated that the social cost of a lack of housing affordability is greater than the social cost of carbon

verification_trace_per_figure.claim: Council created a Temporary Committee on Electrification Policy (TCEP)
verification_trace_per_figure.evidence: feb11_tcep.mp4 visually shows the slide 'Recommended Approach to Policy Development' with bullet 'Establishes Temporary Committee on Electrification Policy (TCEP)'

verification_trace_per_figure.claim: Late May / early June 2026 public hearing on fee adoption
verification_trace_per_figure.source_text: Late May–Early June 2026: Public hearing on fee adoption and associated policies

verification_trace_per_figure.claim: June 3 first reading / June 17 second reading
verification_trace_per_figure.source_text: First reading of the ordinance was scheduled for June 3, 2026, with a second reading on June 17, 2026 (open caveat: re-verify against bend.granicus.com agenda packet before publish)
Proposed Effective Dates: April 1, 2027 / July 1, 2027 / January 1, 2028

verification_trace_per_figure.claim: Bend City Hall, 710 NW Wall Street
verification_trace_per_figure.source_text: City Council Chambers, 710 NW Wall Street, and online

open_caveats: Re-verify the June 3 / June 17 / April 1 2027 dates against the actual Bend City Council agenda packet on bend.granicus.com before public publish.

All meeting clips are excerpted under fair-use commentary doctrine for local news. Each clip is short, used to provide visual context for narration, and credited on screen per broll/manifest.json plus this file. No license fee required for Oregon public records, but recommend an Oregon real-estate attorney review before commercial distribution.
